geopolitics_summary: Record European heat wave amid methane regulation delays; Strait of Hormuz shipping tensions; EU–UK ETS deal negotiations delayed.

themes.headline: EU methane restrictions delayed; half of member states now supportive of postponement
themes.why_it_matters: Delayed methane caps weaken supply-side cost floor for EU gas; lower expected compliance costs reduce upward pressure on wholesale gas and power switching economics. TTF curve relevance.

themes.headline: Record European heat wave drives peak electricity demand and cooling load stress
themes.why_it_matters: Sustained extreme heat increases air-conditioning demand, strains thermal generation via cooling water scarcity (esp. France nuclear), lifts day-ahead and intraday power spreads. DA–ID volatility spike.

themes.headline: Strait of Hormuz ship attack halts evacuation; Iran enforces new shipping corridor rules
themes.why_it_matters: Shipping disruption in Hormuz raises crude price risk and LNG optionality; higher Brent/WTI widens crude-to-gas switching margin, indirectly supporting power-to-gas arbitrage and gas demand.

themes.headline: EU–UK ETS linking negotiations postponed following UK political transition
themes.why_it_matters: Delay in EU–UK emissions trading agreement extends uncertainty over bilateral carbon market linkage; affects EUA arbitrage to ICE and UK ETS allowance pricing; horizon months-long.

themes.headline: UAE exit from OPEC+ reduces cartel crude production and spare capacity
themes.why_it_matters: Smaller OPEC+ effective spare capacity may support higher Brent; elevated crude prices increase competing LNG demand from Asia, tightening northwest European LNG supply and gas curves.

watchlist: EU–UK ETS summit rescheduling (emissions deal timeline uncertain; watch for new date announcement)
French nuclear output monitoring (heat-driven cooling constraints; nuclear hedges CA+1 power curve)
Hormuz shipping developments (geopolitical escalation watch; crude/LNG flow risks)
OPEC+ production signals (spare capacity tightening; Brent support implies LNG arb tightness)
